title: Executive Order 11141—Declaring a Public Policy Against Discrimination on the Basis of Age

flags.justification: A policy declaration applicable to federal contractors; implementation is distributed across department and agency heads rather than concentrated in one official.

The order declares a policy and instructs agency heads to 'take appropriate action to enunciate' it, but specifies no investigation, complaint, or consequence mechanism anywhere for a contractor who actually discriminates — the same enforcement-architecture gap already found in EO 10939.
Single coherent purpose: age-discrimination policy for federal contractors.
No enforcement mechanism exists at all (see Flag 2), so there is no enforcement standard to assess for vagueness specifically.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
Permanence carve-out applies: a non-discrimination policy is evergreen by nature, consistent with EO 8802, EO 9981, and EO 11375.
No existing oversight mechanism is removed.

With no enforcement mechanism specified (Flag 2), no external party is positioned to catch or surface violations either — a distinct finding from the internal-responsibility gap, addressing the absence of any outside monitoring channel.
A genuinely precedent-setting policy declaration — the first federal-contractor-level age-discrimination policy, three years ahead of the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.
The 'bona fide occupational qualification, retirement plan, or statutory requirement' exceptions are standard, recognizable employment-law concepts, not a broad undermining carve-out.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 3/16 = 18.75%. Second clear instance in this corpus of a policy declared with no enforcement architecture at all (see also EO 10939). Worth tracking as a named recurring pattern in the notable-findings log.
